reporting_guide: Report crop damage within 72 hours of the calamity event. Take geo-tagged photographs of affected fields. Contact nearest agriculture officer or insurance company representative. Do not harvest or destroy affected crop before survey.
compensation_process: Surveyor assesses damage using crop cutting experiments or remote sensing. Claim amount calculated based on sum insured and percentage of loss. Amount credited to farmer's bank account within 15-30 days of claim approval.
required_documents: Weather report or calamity notification from government
claim_process: Report loss to insurance company within 72 hours
Intimate loss to bank (for loanee farmers)
Crop survey conducted by insurance surveyor
Submit claim form with supporting documents
Claim assessment and approval by insurance company
Compensation credited to registered bank account

faqs.q: What crops are covered under PMFBY?
faqs.a: All food crops, oilseeds, and annual commercial/horticultural crops notified by state governments are covered under PMFBY.
Farmers pay 2% for Kharif, 1.5% for Rabi food crops, and 5% for commercial/horticultural crops. Remaining premium is shared by Central and State governments.
Sum insured is the guaranteed amount per hectare based on scale of finance or average yield multiplied by minimum support price.

faqs.q: Can I claim for partial crop loss?
faqs.a: Yes, claims can be filed for partial losses when yield falls below the threshold yield specified in the policy.

programs.name: PM Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
programs.description: National crop insurance scheme covering yield losses from natural calamities, pests, and diseases at subsidized premium rates.

programs.name: Weather Based Crop Insurance (WBCIS)
programs.description: Insurance based on weather parameters like rainfall, temperature, and humidity rather than actual yield loss.

programs.name: State Crop Insurance Schemes
programs.description: Various state governments offer additional crop insurance schemes complementing PMFBY with state-specific coverage.
